seoTitle: How to Increase Page Size in Power BI Desktop
metaDescription: Learn how to set custom canvas sizes in Power BI Desktop so your report pages fit the layout your visuals actually need.
intro: The default report canvas in Power BI Desktop doesn't always fit what you're trying to build. This tutorial explains how to access the page size settings and apply a custom canvas size so your report layout matches your design goals.

steps.heading: Open the page size settings
steps.body: Go to the formatting pane for your report page to find the page size options in Power BI Desktop.

steps.heading: Choose or create a custom size
steps.body: Select a preset size or enter custom width and height values to fit the exact layout you want.

steps.heading: Adjust your visuals to the new canvas
steps.body: Resize and reposition your visuals so they take advantage of the larger or differently proportioned page.

steps.heading: Use custom sizes for specific report types
steps.body: Apply larger canvases for detailed departmental dashboards or client-facing reports that need more room for visuals.

faq.question: Will changing the page size affect how the report looks on mobile?
faq.answer: Custom canvas sizes are for the desktop/web layout; you may want to configure a separate mobile layout for phone viewing.

faq.question: Can I set a different page size for each page in the same report?
faq.answer: Yes, page size is set per page, so different pages in one report can use different canvas dimensions.

faq.question: Does a larger canvas slow down the report?
faq.answer: Canvas size itself doesn't affect performance, though adding more visuals to fill a larger page can.

faq.question: Can I go back to the default size after customizing it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can switch back to a standard preset size at any time from the same settings.
